revision.comment: Bot: mark 3 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:智明権現]] {{Infobox deity}}
== Chimyō Gongen == '''Chimyō Gongen''' (智明権現) is a {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} blending {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|qid=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}} and {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} from {{ill|Mount Daisen|en|Mount Daisen|qid=Q715498|lt=Mount Daisen|1=Mount Daisen}}. It is considered the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha|1=Honji suijaku}} (original Buddha) manifestation of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}}. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} (anti-Buddhist movement), Chimyō Gongen was enshrined in various shrines nationwide, having been {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|Daisen-ji|en|Daisen-ji|qid=Q3012163|lt=Daisen-ji|1=Daisen-ji}} in {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|qid=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}. It is also known as Daichimyō Gongen (大智明権現), Daichimyō Daigongen (大智明大権現), Daisen Chimyō Daigongen (大山智明大権現), and Daisen Chimyō Gongen (大仙智明大権現). == Overview == Mount Daisen, regarded as a sacred body in ancient mountain worship, was identified as a manifestation of Jizō Bosatsu. According to legend, {{ill|Shunpō|ja|俊方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from {{ill|Tamatsukuri, Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province 玉造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri, Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Daisen-ji during the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō era|1=Yōrō}} (717–724 CE) period.<ref name="daisenji">[http://www.daisenji.jp/index.php?id=9 Tendaishū Bekkaku Honzan Kakuban-san Daisen-ji - Temple Overview]</ref> The {{ill|Senchūshō|ja|選集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senchūshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recounts how Shunpō accidentally shot what he believed was a deer but turned out to be Jizō Bosatsu. Overcome with remorse, he became a monk, took the name Konren, and established a hermitage to enshrine Jizō Bosatsu. Similar stories are found in the {{ill|Daisen-ji Engi|ja|大山寺縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen-ji Engi|1=Daisen-ji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97184197}}.<ref name="daisenji"/> In {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 1 (767 CE), {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q232955}} conferred the honorary title of Daichimyō Daigongen on the Jizō Bosatsu of Mount Daisen as part of syncretic practices.<ref name="daisenji"/> During the medieval period, Shugendō flourished at Mount Daisen, with {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism|1=Tiantai}} becoming dominant. At its peak between the late Heian and Muromachi periods, Daisen-ji oversaw over 160 temples and shrines and boasted an army of 3,000 {{ill|Sōhei|en|Sōhei|qid=Q1475695|lt=warrior monks|1=Sōhei}}.<ref>[http://yokoso.pref.tottori.jp/dd.aspx?itemid=1705 Tottori Prefecture Tourism Information - Daisen-ji]</ref> During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Chimyō Gongen (associated with Jizō Bosatsu), Reizō Gongen (associated with Kannon Bosatsu), and Riju Gongen (associated with Monju Bosatsu) formed the '''{{ill|Three Deities of Mount Daisen|ja|大山三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Mount Daisen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550940}}'''.<ref>According to the Daisen-ji Engi, Monju, Kannon, and Jizō Bosatsu manifested with Sanskrit characters, seated west, east, and center respectively, forming the Three Deities of Daisen.</ref> == Pilgrimage to Mount Daisen == The belief that Mount Daisen was a resting place for souls of the deceased led to the fusion of Daisen worship and Jizō devotion. Pilgrimages to pray for the repose of the dead at Chimyō Gongen flourished during the Edo period. Today, the {{ill|33 Kannon Pilgrimage of Daisen-ji|ja|大山寺三十三カ所地蔵めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/33 Kannon Pilgrimage of Daisen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550941}} route reflects these historical practices. == Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}-era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} led to the abolition of Shugendō-related deities like Chimyō Gongen. In 1871 (Meiji 4), Daisen-ji was forcibly separated into {{ill|Ōgamiyama Shrine|en|Ōgamiyama Shrine|qid=Q251160|lt=Ōgamiyama Shrine|1=Ōgamiyama Shrine}} and the Tendai sect temple Kakuban-san Daisen-ji. == Temples Enshrining Chimyō Gongen == Though few, some temples continue to enshrine Chimyō Gongen, having survived the anti-Buddhist purges: * {{ill|Kakuban-san Daisen-ji|ja|角磐山大山寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuban-san Daisen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550942|1=Kakuban-san Daisen-ji Temple}}<ref>[http://www.daisenji.jp/ Official Site of Kakuban-san Daisen-ji]</ref><ref>10th temple of the {{ill|Izumo Province Pilgrimage|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Pilgrimage|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395860}}</ref> ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, Saihaku District) * {{ill|Ōgashima-ji|ja|大賀島寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgashima-ji|1=Ōgashima-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11439240}}, Daitōzan ({{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|qid=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama|1=Setouchi, Okayama}}) - Spring Festival for Daichimyō Daigongen * {{ill|Kaizō-ji|ja|海蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizō-ji|1=Kaizō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11559209}}, Busshōzan ({{ill|Kasaoka|en|Kasaoka|qid=Q863739|lt=Kasaoka, Okayama|1=Kasaoka}})<ref>10th temple of the {{ill|Chugoku Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|中国観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chugoku Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550944}}</ref><ref>[http://www.city.kasaoka.okayama.jp/001p/001q.html Official Kasaoka City Website]</ref> * {{ill|Senkō-ji|ja|千光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senkō-ji|1=Senkoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405368}}, Daihōzan ({{ill|Onomichi|en|Onomichi|qid=Q696694|lt=Onomichi, Hiroshima|1=Onomichi}})<ref>[http://www.senkouji.jp/guidance/info011.html Senkō-ji Official Site]</ref> * {{ill|Goryū Sonryū-in|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sonryū-in|1=Goryū Sontakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372540}} ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama|1=Kurashiki}}) - Daisen Chimyō Daigongen == Mantra == '''On Kakaka Bisanmaei Sowaka''' (オン カカカ ビサンマエイ ソワカ) == References == {{reflist|2|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Goryū Sonryū-in|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sonryū-in|1=Goryū Sontakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372540}} {{Buddhism2|qq=}} {{Shinto|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|智明権現|version=99924117|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:智明権現]]
